title: Lush Egyptian Chinese Cabbage Tagine
description: A briny, flaky, romantic Egyptian dish featuring Chinese cabbage in a miso-marinated sauce, served in a traditional tagine pot.
instructions: Cut the cabbage into bite-sized wedges and place in a bowl.
Whisk miso, soy sauce, rice vinegar, maple syrup, cumin, coriander, garlic, lemon juice, sea salt, and sesame oil until smooth.
Pour the miso mixture over the cabbage, toss to coat evenly, and let marinate for 20 minutes.
Heat a tagine pot over medium heat and add a splash of water to prevent sticking.
Add the marinated cabbage to the pot, cover, and simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Check for tenderness; if needed, add 1 tbsp water and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Remove the lid, let the dish rest for 5 minutes, then gently fluff with a fork.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro or toasted sesame seeds if desired.
